findings.title: Duplicate entries in forbidden_rollback_behavior_claims (inherited, harmless)
findings.details: Within the added control_plane_permission_audit_binding_rollback_behavior_definition.forbidden_rollback_behavior_claims array, 'write_human_approval_record' and 'write_human_approval' each appear twice (once in the review/Claude section, again in the approval section). This is inherited from concatenating the upstream review-required + approval-required + blocked-claim forbidden lists (via CONTROL_PLANE_PERMISSION_AUDIT_BINDING_BLOCKED_CLAIM_OUTPUT_POLICY.forbiddenBlockedClaimOutputClaims spread in policies.js) and is faithfully mirrored by the policy, so there is no contract/policy drift and the strict JSON.stringify equality check still passes. It is functionally harmless because precheckControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRollbackBehavior builds a Set from the list before checking. Non-blocking; only worth de-duplicating for list hygiene. Line number is approximate (inside the large added contract block).

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Scope confirmed metadata-only Risk A: precheck returns a frozen receipt, every *_permitted/*_write_permitted flag is false, and it fails closed on any truthy known-forbidden OR unknown claim (Object.entries loop throws on non-false values).

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: No-write / no-runtime guarantees hold: no rollback/retry/compensation execution, no operator/customer notification, no incident-trace, observability, audit, database, storage, product-state, permission-engine/AuthZ, review/approval/blocked-output, or Claude-runtime writes are produced; forbidden-claim and fail_closed_on lists enumerate all of these.

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Input guards are correct: requires permission_audit_binding entrypoint, blocked_claim_output_subphase_id === RP00.P02.M05.S16, present blocked_claim_output_receipt + non-empty audit_event_ref, and full assertControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingBlockedClaimOutputResult re-check before deriving S17 metadata.

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Double-application is implicitly prevented: an S17 output fed back as input fails the embedded S16 re-validation because next_subphase (S18) and working_product_behavior_ref differ from S16 expectations.

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Drift checks clean: RESULT_FIELDS (policies.js) == contract result_fields == fixture result_fields; rollback_strategy_refs/entries, required receipt fields, decision/scope values, and required_input_fields (== S16 result fields) are identical across policy, contract definition, fixture, and validator-script expectations; object_id fallback ('firm_level_permission_audit_binding') matches between precheck and assert.

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Validator-script wiring verified: weighted boundary (non-terminal, next S18), closeout_requirements flag control_plane_permission_audit_binding_rollback_behavior_verified, closeout_state_policy CP00-013/docs/closeout-packs/cp00-013, requiredClosedSubphases advanced to include S16, current subphase S17, plus README/test text anchors all asserted.

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Security/goal boundary: synthetic-only tenant/actor/matter with tenant-consistent ids, no_real_data true, additive change does not touch G00-G30/HRX goal boundary; human_approval_boundary_preserved gate retained.

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Missing CP00-013 manifest/command-evidence/claude-review-result/adjudication/construction-inspection are expected pre-review state; the RP00 validator's strict JSON.stringify(result)==fixture.precheck_result equality must already be passing since the script is reported to fail only on those missing evidence files, corroborating fixture/result parity.

non_blocking_notes.title: Claude review note
non_blocking_notes.description: Minor observation (not a finding): assertControlPlanePermissionAuditBindingRollbackBehaviorResult has no direct negative unit test for a tampered result (e.g. a flipped *_permitted flag), though it is exercised on the happy path via the precheck and the validator script; risk is low since such a result cannot be produced by the precheck.

adjudication_notes: One completed claude-opus-4-8 max read-only/no-tools review is recorded for CP00-013.
Claude returned PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with zero P0 and zero P1 findings.

The single raw P2 duplicate-forbidden-claim hygiene finding is explicitly deferred because the runtime prechecker uses a Set, the contract mirrors the policy, and changing implementation after this completed review would alter the reviewed diff without reducing a blocking risk.
The expected pre-evidence RP00 failure is resolved by creating this CP00-013 evidence and rerunning final validation.
